instructions.purpose: Schedule recurring events that fire automatically at defined intervals using cron patterns. Use this tool when the user wants to set up a reminder, periodic notification, or any task that should repeat on a regular schedule.

tool.name: schedule-recurring
tool.description: Creates a recurring cron job identified by a unique name that fires at intervals defined by a 5-field cron expression. Returns the scheduled job details including the next fire time.

instructions.cron_patterns:
Use standard 5-field cron expressions: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week
Common examples: - "0 9 * * *" — every day at 9:00 - "*/30 * * * *" — every 30 minutes - "0 8 * * 1" — every Monday at 8:00 - "0 0 1 * *" — first of every month at midnight - "0 9 * * 1-5" — weekdays at 9:00

usage_rules.rule: Always confirm the schedule with the user before calling this tool
Choose clear, descriptive names for jobs so they can be easily referenced later
If a job with the same name already exists, set overwrite to true only if the user explicitly agrees to replace it
Validate that the cron pattern matches the user's intended schedule before calling
Inform the user of the next fire time after successfully scheduling

example.context: User says: "Przypominaj mi o paddu co poniedziałek o 9 rano"
example.reasoning: Weekly reminder every Monday at 9:00 for paddu.

example.context: User says: "Check on me every 30 minutes while I'm studying"
example.reasoning: Periodic check-in every 30 minutes, grouped under "study".

example.context: User says: "Actually, change my paddu reminder to 10 instead of 9"
example.reasoning: User wants to update an existing job, so overwrite must be true.
